summary: This notebook demonstrates a complete pipeline for converting text descriptions into SVG drawings using Stable Diffusion as an intermediate bitmap generator. It introduces a custom metric package to evaluate both the generated bitmaps and final SVGs against multiple-choice QA pairs, OCR, and aesthetic scores. The author shows how to adapt Stable Diffusion prompts with specific prefixes, suffixes, and negative prompts to enforce a flat, minimalistic style suitable for vector conversion. Key takeaways include the effectiveness of targeted prompt engineering for style control, the viability of contour-based SVG generation from diffusion outputs, and how the custom metric decomposes visual quality into granular components.
competition_flow: Loads text descriptions and QA data, generates intermediate bitmaps via Stable Diffusion, converts them to SVGs using contour extraction and simplification, and evaluates them using a custom multi-component scoring metric.
data_reading: Loads train.csv and questions.parquet via kagglehub.competition_download.
Parses JSON fields in the parquet file into lists/dicts and merges them into a single pandas DataFrame.
data_processing: Resizes images to 384x384 for metric evaluation.
Converts PIL images to numpy arrays.
Applies k-means color quantization and contour detection.
Simplifies polygon coordinates via rounding and point reduction.
features_engineering: Hierarchical contour extraction, k-means color quantization, and importance-weighted polygon simplification for SVG generation.
insights: Prompt engineering with specific prefixes, suffixes, and negative prompts effectively controls Stable Diffusion to produce flat, minimalistic images ideal for SVG conversion.
Contour-based SVG generation with importance-weighted simplification preserves visual fidelity while managing file size constraints.
The custom metric successfully decomposes image quality into VQA, OCR, and aesthetic components, providing a granular evaluation of generative outputs.
